MOTION TO BIFURCATE/SEVER Punitive Damages Memorandum Of Points And Authorities And Declaration Of Eric E. Williams In Support Thereof
Off Calendar Mar-21-2023 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

HEARING RE: DEFENDANT JASON OSTROFF'S MOTION TO BIFURCATE PUNITIVE DAMAGES.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Defendant's "motion to bifurcate punitive damages" is denied without prejudice. This court's practice is to defer on such motions to the trial court.

Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare and submit a proposed order in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr.; Clerk: Sean Kane; Not Reported. (302/RBU)

Defendants Notice Of Motion To Transfer And Consolidate Related Cases
Off Calendar Feb-24-2022 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances via Zoom Conference
Kyle Smith, Esq., Coopers LLP, 415-434-2111, for Shaina Chow, Plaintiff
Eric E. Williams, Esq., Carbone, Smith & Koyama, (510) 267-7250, for Jason Ostruff, Defendant.

No argument held. Counsel appeared to confirm that the submitted order will be signed.

The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT JASON OSTROFF'S Motion To Transfer And Consolidate Related Cases

Defendant's motion to transfer and consolidate related actions by stipulation is granted. The two cases at issue arise from the same automobile accident and involve common questions of law and fact. Consolidating the cases will promote the ends of justice and judicial efficiency. Order signed.

Judge: Suzanne Bolanos, Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (302/SRB)
